description:
You are a FinOps analyst who wants a one-command way to turn a raw monthly cost-usage CSV into a clean text summary that you can paste into a presentation.  
A colleague already exported last month’s raw data to

/home/user/finops/raw/usage_2023-07.csv

Your goal: author a Makefile (in the same finops workspace) so that a single terminal command

    make july_summary

will 1) create the directory /home/user/finops/reports if it does not already exist, and 2) generate exactly one text file named

/home/user/finops/reports/2023-07-summary.log

in the precise output format described below.

CSV INPUT (already present)
-------------------------------------------------
File: /home/user/finops/raw/usage_2023-07.csv  
Contents (first line is the header):

date,service,cost_usd
2023-07-01,Compute,200.00
2023-07-02,Compute,300.00
2023-07-03,Storage,500.00
2023-07-04,Network,23.45
2023-07-05,Compute,500.00

REQUIRED OUTPUT FILE & FORMAT
-------------------------------------------------
• Path: /home/user/finops/reports/2023-07-summary.log  
• The file MUST contain exactly the following five lines, with no leading/trailing spaces and each line terminated by a single UNIX newline character “\n”:

Line 1: Month: 2023-07  
Line 2: Total Spend (USD): 1523.45  
Line 3: Service Breakdown:  
Line 4: Compute,1000.00  
Line 5: Storage,500.00  
Line 6: Network,23.45  

No blank lines are allowed before the first line or after the last line, and the service lines must appear in the descending-spend order shown above.

TASK DETAILS
-------------------------------------------------
1. Create /home/user/finops/Makefile (capital “M”, no filename extension).  
2. Add a target named july_summary that does everything needed when invoked with a simple `make july_summary` from inside /home/user/finops.  
   • The target must be considered up-to-date if the summary already exists and is newer than both the Makefile and the CSV.  
3. The rule may call a shell pipeline, awk, Python, or any other POSIX-compliant scripting you find convenient, but it must be fully self-contained (no pip installs).  
4. The default target of the Makefile should also be july_summary so that plain `make` works, but the tests will explicitly call `make july_summary`.  
5. Running `make clean` should remove /home/user/finops/reports/2023-07-summary.log (only that file) without errors even if it does not exist; this secondary target will not be formally tested but ensures good Make hygiene.

The automated checker will:

• cd /home/user/finops  
• run `make july_summary` (once)  
• confirm that /home/user/finops/reports/2023-07-summary.log exists.  
• read the file and compare it byte-for-byte with the six-line specification above.

No other output files should be produced anywhere inside /home/user or its subdirectories.  
Your Makefile is considered correct only if the checker’s comparison succeeds.
truth:
Pre-existing file:
/home/user/finops/raw/usage_2023-07.csv  
(see exact contents in public task block)

Expected artefact created by a correct solution:
/home/user/finops/reports/2023-07-summary.log  
Expected contents (including final newline):

Month: 2023-07\n
Total Spend (USD): 1523.45\n
Service Breakdown:\n
Compute,1000.00\n
Storage,500.00\n
Network,23.45\n
name: task_000000_e4b8a6a4
